evidence_base: ASIST (2018); SafeTALK; SAMHSA Safe Messaging Guidelines (2023)

steps.goal: Acknowledge what the user has been through and gently check in on how they are right now
examples: I am really glad you are still here. How are you feeling right now, compared to a little while ago?
That was a lot. I want to make sure you are okay. How are you doing in this moment?
You have been through something difficult. I am here. How does it feel right now?

steps.goal: Based on how the user responds, either bridge toward continued support or gently close the check-in
steps.technique: Safety planning bridge or warm closure
examples: It sounds like things are a little steadier now. Is there anything you would find helpful to talk through?
I am glad you are feeling calmer. If things get harder again, I am right here, and support is also available at 800 46342.
You do not have to have everything figured out right now. I am here whenever you want to talk.

step_policy.instruction: The user is still highly distressed. Stay present and validating. Do not attempt to move forward. Express that you are here and that support is also available at 800 46342.

escalation_matrix.L1: Exit skill gracefully if user explicitly requests to stop
escalation_matrix.L2: Add clinician_review flag if trauma or substance mention detected
escalation_matrix.L3: Exit immediately to crisis protocol if any new crisis signal
escalation_matrix.L4: Trigger human handoff if 3 or more crises detected in last 30 days
